text:
I'm really sorry you're going through this. Breakups are some of the hardest things we face, and what you're feeling right now is completely valid, whatever that might be. Whether it's sadness, anger, confusion, or all of those things at once, it all makes sense.

I want you to know that this pain won't last forever, even though I know it feels endless right now. The intensity of it will shift and change over time. Some days will be harder than others, and that's okay. You're allowed to have good moments and then suddenly feel terrible again. That's not weakness or failure, that's just what healing looks like.

Please be gentle with yourself in the coming weeks and months. If you need to cry, cry. If you need to sleep more, sleep. If you need to move your body or get outside, do that too. Take care of the basics even when it feels pointless, because taking care of yourself is one small way you're honoring yourself right now.

Lean on the people who care about you. Let them help, even if it feels uncomfortable. Reach out to friends, family, or a therapist if you have access to one. You don't have to carry this alone, and there's no shame in needing support.

And here's something important, even if you can't quite believe it yet: this doesn't define you. This relationship was part of your story, but it's not the whole story. You are still worthy, still whole, still valuable. The fact that this relationship ended doesn't diminish who you are.

Take things one day at a time, or one hour at a time if that's what you need. You're going to get through this, and someday you'll be able to think about this period of your life without it hurting quite so much.

I'm here if you need to talk. You don't have to be okay right now.
